raw_consumer_statement:
In XXXX, I discovered fraudulent charges on my Barclays XXXX credit card ending in acct # XXXX totaling approximately {$2300.00}. I contacted Barclays Bank, and it provided me with provisional credit pending its investigation. On XX/XX/XXXX, I received several letters from Barclays Bank rescinding my provisional credit for the fraud stating there was no fraud committed. On XX/XX/XXXX, I called Barclays XXXX Department and spoke to a representative named XXXX. I told him my son is an XXXX, and I recently discovered that he had stolen information from XXXX of my credit cards, and used them fraudulently without my knowledge or consent. XXXX advised me to file a police report and fax it to the XXXX department, which I did the next day. I also faxed a copy of the my Order of Protection against my XXXX, XXXX XXXX with a stay away order preventing him from coming into my residence. On, XX/XX/XXXX, I received a letter from Barclays Bank in which it stated that it " ... wanted to provide an update on your recent reassertion fraud claim. We have completed a detailed review of your claim and, based on the information available, have concluded that the transactions you identified are not fraudulent. '' When, I received this letter, I called Barclays XXXX department. I requested to speak to a XXXX. When I asked the XXXX why my claim was denied she stated : It is Barclays Bank 's policy to deny XXXX claims between family members, and my only recourse to recoup my money was to sue my XXXX for the fraud. I wrote a letter and emailed it to Barclays Bank XXXX Department under its new case number XXXX in which I attached XXXX ( XXXX ) exhibits ; Exhibit XXXXLetters from Barclays reversing provisioal credit for fraud ; Exhibit XXXX Police Report ; Exhibit XXXXOrder of Protection against my XXXX, XXXX XXXX, who committed the fraud without my permission, consent or knowledge ; Exhibit XXXX-Incident Report filed because my XXXX violated the stay away order. 
I ended this letter stating to Barclays : " I would like to request that you review and investigate my fraud claim once more. It is clear that under the FCBA, if my XXXX used my credit card without my knowledge or permission, it is considered unauthorized use, and my fraud claim should be granted. '' I can't see how Barclays can adopt a policy that brazenly and openly violates the FCBA.
